title: Shipping as a Habit: The Linear Method

segments.label: Introduction
segments.summary: The fallacy of the infinite backlog. Why most teams feel like they are running in place.

segments.label: The Cycle Framework
segments.summary: Defining cycles vs. sprints. How a fixed cadence creates momentum and reduces decision fatigue.

segments.label: Scoping and Precision
segments.summary: The art of saying no. How to scope features so they actually ship within a cycle.

segments.label: The Culture of Shipping
segments.summary: Building a team that values momentum over perfection. The psychological impact of 'Done'.

segments.label: Closing/Q&A
segments.summary: Practical steps to transition your team to the Linear Method tomorrow.

tone_notes: Direct, authoritative, zero fluff. Focus on the 'why' behind the 'how'. Respect the listener's time.

argument_preservation.adaptation: Intro segment focusing on the 'fallacy of the infinite backlog'.
Deep dive in segment 2 regarding fixed cadence and momentum.

title: Product Management Without the Overhead

segments.label: The Process Debt
segments.summary: Why traditional PM tools make you slower, not faster. The cost of 'Management for Management's sake'.

segments.label: Opinionated Software
segments.summary: Why Linear doesn't try to be everything to everyone. The power of a specific workflow.

segments.label: Automating the Routine
segments.summary: Using tool defaults to eliminate status updates and manual tracking.

segments.label: High-Performance Teams
segments.summary: Case studies of teams that doubled velocity by simplifying their issue tracking.

segments.label: Conclusion
segments.summary: Final thoughts on intentionality in tool selection and workflow design.

tone_notes: Minimalist, engineer-centric, opinionated. High signal-to-noise ratio.

argument_preservation.adaptation: The 'Opinionated Software' segment discussing why specific workflows beat generic flexibility.
High-performance team case studies highlighting velocity through simplicity.
